topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp white paper felt like sandpaper under my fingertips as I unfolded the document. Every line, every carefully chosen anecdote about my life, was a calculated fabrication. My jaw tightened so hard I thought my teeth might crack. I reread the passage detailing my supposed 'pivotal moment' in college, the description so vivid it felt like a physical assault. A slow, burning heat began to rise in my chest, spreading outwards until my palms were slick with sweat. I crumpled the paper into a tight ball, the sound sharp in the otherwise silent apartment. "This is a lie," I whispered, the sound brittle and strained. I stood up, pacing the small room, my movements jerky and agitated. Every shadow seemed to mock me, and I couldn't seem to settle my breathing.

[story 2]

I stared at the printed pages, the ink mocking me with its deceptive elegance. My hands shook uncontrollably as I traced the fabricated timeline, each word a deliberate misdirection. A low, guttural sound escaped my throat, a sound more animal than human. I slammed the folder onto the desk, the resulting thud echoing loudly in the office. My eyes darted around the room, searching for an escape route, anything to dispel the suffocating pressure building behind my sternum. I felt a fierce, hot surge of anger bubbling up, demanding release. I gripped the edge of the desk until my knuckles turned white, fighting the urge to shout, to shatter something.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow. I sat motionless, the world tilting slightly as the carefully constructed narrative dissolved into nonsense. My breath hitched, shallow and fast. A tremor ran through my hands, making it difficult to hold the document steady. I looked up, my gaze sharp and cold, directed at the empty space where the author must have been. A deep, simmering resentment began to coil in my gut, a heavy, unpleasant weight. I pushed my chair back violently, scraping it across the floor. I didn't speak; the sheer force of my internal turmoil rendered words useless, leaving only a tense, vibrating stillness in the air.
